characters.description: A returning peak two-star fugitive whose face has become unsafe to show. After a life-affinity spirit permanently reconstructs his appearance, he heads toward the Northern Belt with Bloodshed in search of a place where imperial power has less reach.
characters.name: Freddy Stern

characters.description: Freddy's sentient, blood-linked companion. Bloodshed helps him locate and negotiate with the life-affinity remnant that permanently alters his face.
characters.name: Bloodshed

characters.description: A peak two-star criminal businessman in the Northern Belt. He hires the newly arrived Freddy as a bodyguard and arranges a forged local identity for him, while concealing the full nature of his operations.
characters.name: Jeffrey

characters.description: The Northern Belt identity created for Freddy Stern after his face is permanently changed. The papers present him as a forty-seven-year-old local, giving the fugitive a name he can use for housing, work, and public dealings.
characters.name: Freddy Cliff

characters.description: A disgraced former four-star lord reduced to three stars by imperial punishment. Facing exile in the Northern Belt, Basilisk uses knowledge of a dangerous secret to compel Mark and Nahar to travel with him.
characters.name: Basilisk

characters.description: A three-star associate tied to the protection of two square-pupiled body snatchers. Basilisk's threat of imperial exposure forces him to join the disgraced lord's Northern Belt exile party, despite the danger surrounding their hidden dependents.
characters.name: Mark

characters.description: Mark's co-guardian of two dangerous, square-pupiled body snatchers. Nahar reluctantly accompanies Basilisk into exile because disclosure of the hidden pair would bring imperial punishment, leaving the travelers bound together by mutual risk rather than trust.
characters.name: Nahar

characters.description: Freddy's critically injured partner, kept alive as a disembodied head while the possibility of restoration narrows. Freddy commits most of his available money to an uncertain attempt to reconstruct her body.
characters.name: Sophia

characters.description: An impoverished mortal who uses his late brother's gear while seeking a prime vestige. Freddy approaches him at the passage hub and invites him into the interspace.
characters.name: Lucas Black

characters.description: The three-star lightning user who leads Valhalla, a Northern Belt faction known for attacking criminal operations. Freddy identifies Thor as the local leader most likely to become a useful and acceptable ally.
characters.name: Thor

characters.description: Lucas's mother, recovered after loan sharks hold and beat her over gambling debt. After receiving medical and dental care, she moves into Freddy's home with Lucas.
characters.name: Helen Black

characters.description: A two-star operative from a powerful local business family and Helen's former partner. He confronts Freddy over the destroyed loan operation and begins probing which faction, if any, stands behind the newcomer.
characters.name: Jason Jem

characters.description: Thor's close ally and a member of Valhalla. Travis comforts Thor after the leader accepts a lasting soul injury rather than submit to imperial removal from the Northern Belt.
characters.name: Travis

characters.description: The imperial official who pressures Thor to leave the Northern Belt as his advancement approaches the prohibited threshold. The adjudicator represents the Empire's power to remove rising three-stars from the region.
characters.name: The imperial adjudicator

characters.description: The name Freddy gives the fortress dungeon's gate constructs when he enters the challenge.
characters.name: Slave

characters.description: A young delver whose unusual soul construct can reshape ambient aura in the nether echo and draw ether from the result. Freddy recognizes that the ability could attract dangerous attention and seeks a voluntary connection with her group.
characters.name: Janice

characters.description: Jason's brother, a shadow-affinity delver from the same debt-exploitation family. During the fortress siege, Sean holds captured invaders to delay the next enemy wave.
characters.name: Sean Jem

characters.description: A scarred older Valhalla fighter assigned to the planned raid on an organ-trafficking operation. No connection to the earlier employer of the same name is established.
characters.name: Jeffrey

characters.description: A red-armored Valhalla fighter assigned to the planned raid on a Strata-backed organ-trafficking site.
characters.name: The red-armored Valhalla fighter

characters.description: The captured lead doctor at a Strata-backed organ-trafficking site and the physician scheduled to oversee Sophia's reconstruction. Freddy intervenes when Thor prepares to execute him because Sophia's treatment depends on his work.
characters.name: Sophia's lead doctor

characters.description: Valhalla's therapist, whose dream affinity gives his voice a calming effect. Noah assesses Freddy and explains that treatment cannot change his beliefs or conduct unless he chooses to change them.
characters.name: Noah

characters.description: The operator of a guarded organ-harvesting business connected to Strata. His organization agrees to buy repeated sets of organs from Freddy through secret exchanges and an anonymous account.
characters.name: The Strata owner's son

characters.description: A respected Valhalla scout and ranged fighter who publicly challenges Thor's protection of Freddy. His criticism exposes growing resentment inside the faction over the resources and trust given to its dangerous outsider.
characters.name: Charlie King

characters.description: A member of the Jem family investigating the deaths, disappearances, and illicit trade surrounding her relatives. Gina and her brother suspect that three hidden operatives are connected to Valhalla, though their proposed links remain unproven.
characters.name: Gina Jem

characters.description: An unidentified client who makes regular high-value sales through a Strata broker. The Jem family's proposed connection between Skull and Valhalla remains unverified.
characters.name: Skull

characters.description: Co-owner and chief executive of the Jem family business, as well as Gina's father. After hearing Helen's accusation against Valhalla, he accepts her account, promises to recover Lucas, and brings in a powerful outsider to address the family's conflict.
characters.name: Keegan Jem

characters.description: An unidentified visitor near the peak of the third star whom Keegan Jem presents as a powerful solution to the family's problem.
characters.name: The unidentified visitor

characters.description: An unidentified high-level operative hired by Keegan Jem to kill the primary target in an imminent attack on Valhalla.
characters.name: The armored assassin

characters.description: An ether construct bound to truth and embodying primordial insanity. Appearing as the host of Freddy's completed isolation challenge, Insanity explains the reward process and presents spirits for upgrading his abilities.
characters.name: Insanity
